output: All 4 batch-2 tasks (T2, T5, T9, T13) executed successfully. Added 16 normalization-helper tests to test_topic_editor_core.py (all pass). Updated post_sectioned_topic tool schema with blocks property and relaxed required array. Extended TOPIC_EDITOR_SYSTEM_PROMPT with Structured Document Topics guidance. Updated _summary_for_tool to store blocks alongside body/sections. Added render_topic_publish_units function with deterministic publish-unit ordering, inline per-block citations with Discord jump URLs, and no global Sources footer. Added _resolve_media_url_from_metadata helper. Updated _message_payload with compact media_refs_available and guild_id. Full test suite: 117 passed (71 topic_editor + 44 related + 2 vision_clients), 0 failures, 0 regressions.

task_updates.executor_notes: Added 16 tests in 5 test classes to test_topic_editor_core.py. TestNormalizeMediaRef: 10 tests covering shorthand→canonical, canonical pass-through, embed refs, invalid kind, missing message_id, non-dict, non-integer indices. TestNormalizeDocumentBlocks: 8 tests covering legacy body→intro, legacy sections→section blocks, topic-level source fallback, new-style blocks preservation, invalid type skipping, body→text fallback, empty summary. TestNormalizeTopicDocument, TestBlockHelpers, TestCollectDocumentSourceIds: coverage for wrapper, distinct union, order preservation. All pass.

Updated post_sectioned_topic tool schema: added blocks array property with {type, title, text, source_message_ids, media_refs} sub-schema; relaxed required array from [proposed_key, headline, body, sections, source_message_ids] to [proposed_key, headline, body, source_message_ids] (sections no longer required when blocks present). Updated tool description to document structured blocks usage, canonical media-ref shape, shorthand acceptance, no global footer rule. Extended TOPIC_EDITOR_SYSTEM_PROMPT with 'Structured Document Topics' section describing 6 rules: per-block sources, per-block media, canonical shape, no global footer, block types, stable refs. Updated _summary_for_tool to store blocks alongside body/sections when provided.

Added render_topic_publish_units(topic, source_metadata=None) function. Returns ordered units [{kind: 'text', content}, {kind: 'media', url, ref}]. Deterministic order: header+intro text with inline [[1]](url) citations → intro media → each section text with inline citations → that section media. Citations per block deduped and ordered by first appearance. Jump URLs use https://discord.com/channels/{guild_id}/{channel_id}/{message_id}. No global Sources footer. Falls back to legacy render_topic when no blocks present. Added _resolve_media_url_from_metadata helper for URL resolution from attachment/embed metadata. Existing render_topic unchanged.

Updated _message_payload to include compact media_refs_available (matching get_topic_editor_message_context output shape) with {kind, index, url_present, content_type, filename} for both attachments and embeds. Added guild_id field to payload. Payload remains bounded (no raw URLs in media_refs_available). Embeds indexed with kind='embed'; attachments with kind='attachment'. Existing media_urls field preserved for backwards compatibility.

sense_check_acknowledgments.question: Do tests cover both shorthand {message_id, attachment_index} and canonical {message_id, kind, index} media refs?
sense_check_acknowledgments.verdict: PASS. test_shorthand_attachment_index_converts_to_canonical and test_shorthand_with_string_index cover shorthand→canonical conversion. test_canonical_attachment_ref_passes_through and test_canonical_embed_ref_passes_through cover canonical pass-through for both attachment and embed kinds.

sense_check_acknowledgments.question: Does the updated tool schema accept blocks input and does the required array allow blocks as alternative to sections?
sense_check_acknowledgments.verdict: PASS. The post_sectioned_topic input_schema now includes a blocks property with {type, title, text, source_message_ids, media_refs} sub-schema. The required array is [proposed_key, headline, body, source_message_ids] — sections is no longer required, allowing blocks-only calls through.

sense_check_acknowledgments.question: Does render_topic_publish_units produce ordered units with inline citations per block and no global Sources footer?
sense_check_acknowledgments.verdict: PASS. render_topic_publish_units produces ordered [{kind: 'text', content}, {kind: 'media', ...}] units. Intro block gets header prefix, section blocks get title if present. Each block's text content is followed by inline [[1]](url) citations (deduped per block, ordered by first appearance). Media units follow immediately after their owning block's text. No 'Source:' or 'Sources:' suffix is appended anywhere in the function.

sense_check_acknowledgments.question: Does _message_payload output include media_refs_available with {kind, index, url_present, content_type, filename} for attachments and embeds?
sense_check_acknowledgments.verdict: PASS. _message_payload now builds media_refs_available as a list of compact dicts. Attachments get {kind: 'attachment', index: idx, url_present: bool, content_type, filename}. Embeds get {kind: 'embed', index: embed_idx, url_present: bool, content_type: None, filename: None}. Shape matches get_topic_editor_message_context output.
